nodes.name: 大麻鳽
nodes.summary: 大麻鳽（Eurasian bittern，学名 Botaurus stellaris），分类上属于鹈形目，并归入鹭科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于黑龙江省，常见栖息地包括湿地、沼泽，主要威胁包括湿地退化、人类活动扰动、湿地排水。

nodes.name: 黑龙江省
nodes.summary: 黑龙江省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括朱鹮、大麻鳽、苍眉蝗莺，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地排水
nodes.summary: 湿地被排干转为农业或建设用地。

nodes.name: 鹭科
nodes.summary: 鹭科（Ardeidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联57种鸟类，如白冠虎斑鷺、横纹虎鹭、船嘴鹭等57种鸟类。

nodes.name: 湿地退化
nodes.summary: 湿地水文和植被结构被改变导致适宜生境下降。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 鹈形目
nodes.summary: 鹈形目（Pelecaniformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联93种鸟类，如朱鹮、黑脸琵鹭、Plumbeous ibis等93种鸟类。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: More northerly populations usually migrate to warmer regions but some birds often remain; birds in northern Europe tend to move south and west to southern Europe, northern and central Africa, and northern Asian birds migrate to parts of the Arabian peninsula, the Indian sub-continent, and the provinces of Heilongjiang, Jilin and Inner Mongolia in eastern China. Outside the breeding season it has less restrictive habitat requirements, and as well as living in reed beds, it visits rice fields, watercress beds, fish farms, gravel pits, sewage works, ditches, flooded areas and marshes. The subspecies B

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: With its specific habitat requirements and the general reduction in wetlands across its range, the population is thought to be in decline globally

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Small resident populations also breed in Morocco, Algeria and Tunisia. It typically inhabits reed beds (Phragmites) and swamps, as well as lakes, lagoons and sluggish rivers fringed by rank vegetation

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The chief threat the bird faces is destruction of reed beds and drainage and disturbance of its wetland habitats. It is one of the species to which the Agreement on the Conservation of African-Eurasian Migratory Waterbirds (AEWA) applies. The southern race has suffered catastrophic decline during the 20th century due to wetland degradation and, unlike the northern race, is of high conservation concern. In the United Kingdom the species became extinct in the late 1800s, and was not recorded to successfully breed again until 1911
